title: Guest Blog: Tomb Raider and the Temples of Angkor – Tomb Raider Horizons
highlights: Although tourism was already growing in Cambodia – and at Angkor specifically – before Tomb Raider was filmed, it was still not a particularly popular destination for travellers. But with the heavily-promoted 2001 release of the film, people across the world were suddenly exposed to this long-ignored site, and this likely had an important influence on tourism to the region. title: Exploring the Tomb Raider Film Set in Cambodia
highlights:
Tomb Raider, has captivated audiences worldwide with its thrilling adventures and stunning  ...    adventurous archaeologist, Lara Croft. Many memorable scenes were shot at the famous Angkor Wat temple complex, which  ...    Angkor Wat, known for its monumental architecture and intricate bas-reliefs, played a significant role in the visual storytelling of Tomb Raider. The film utilized the temple’s ancient beauty to highlight Lara’s complex character and her quest for lost artifacts. The lush jungles surrounding the temples not only enhanced the visual experience but also showcased the natural beauty of Cambodia. Today, many fans of the franchise travel to Cambodia to explore the same sites featured in the film. This fascination with the locale has undoubtedly contributed to the increased tourism in the area, ensuring that the legacy of Tomb Raider continues to inspire adventure seekers and film enthusiasts alike. ## Related Posts

title: Angkor Park 2025 Visitor Data & Tourism Strategy
highlights: saw 955,131 international tourists in 2025, a 6.7% drop. New visa-free policies aim to boost  ...    Cambodia's famed Angkor Archaeological Park welcomed a total of 955,131 international visitors in 2025, a year-on-year decrease of 6.7 per cent, the site's operator said on Thursday. The UNESCO-listed world heritage site earned 44.7 million US dollars in revenue from ticket sales last year, also down 6.5 per cent, said the press release from the state-owned Angkor Enterprise. Main sources of foreign tourists to the park were the United States, Britain, France, China and South Korea.
